vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Driver board bay, power/signal separation along the arm

verify.value: Primary locating faces on Bearing Seat and Reducer Dummy are visible and labeled.
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible.
Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels.
Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links.
Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races.
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range.
cad_brief.mass_est_g is within subsystem specification.

risk.desc: Bearing preload not explicitly defined, potential for premature wear or binding.
risk.mit: Specify bearing preload torque during assembly and include locking mechanism.

risk.desc: Cable routing through tight bends may lead to chafing or damage.
risk.mit: Use flexible conduit or strain relief at bend points.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic
physics_paths: Signal cables: Base connector -> J1 -> J2 -> ... -> J6 -> Wrist connector
Power cables: Base connector -> J1 -> J2 -> ... -> J6 -> Wrist connector
Pneumatic lines: Base connector -> J1 -> J2 -> ... -> J6 -> Wrist connector
integration_opportunities: Coordinate with joint module designers to ensure sufficient clearance for cable routing and slack loops.
Explore the use of modular cable harnesses for easier replacement and maintenance.

manufacturing_strategy.support: Minimal support structures required due to open design.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection, dimensional check, functional test.

verification_focus: Cable routing and slack loop functionality

generated_parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
generated_parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

requirements: Replaceable motor modules per joint with fastener access

verify.value: Motor modules are removable without full teardown.
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Harnesses are routed through visible raceways.
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Access is represented by geometry: fastener pattern.
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief geometry and bend radius are present at cable exits.
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors are reachable and have keying.
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Joint axis rings are visible.
verify.rule: [axis_datum_readability] good: J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible. | bad: Arm links are generic cylinders with no joint service evidence.

verify.value: Cable spine is inspectable.
verify.rule: [cable_spine_serviceability] good: Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels. | bad: Cable routing is buried inside closed links.

verify.value: Joint cartridges are removable.
verify.rule: [joint_cartridge_replaceability] good: Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links. | bad: Reducers/bearings are fused into structural links.

verify.value: Bearings have seats, shoulders, and fit intent.
verify.rule: [bearing_shaft_mounting_sanity] good: Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races. | bad: Bearing dummies float in links with no seat, shoulder, retaining ring groove, or axial location scheme.

verify.value: Harness has slack loops and strain relief.
verify.rule: [moving_joint_harness_workmanship] good: Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range. | bad: Cables cross joints taut, pass through pinch zones, or have no relief at link entry/exit.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Cable chafing risk at sharp edges.
risk.mit: Use edge radii and protective sleeving.

risk.desc: Bearing preload variation due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement torque control during assembly and periodic inspection.

verification_focus: Cable harness strain relief and bend radius

generated_parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
generated_parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

requirements: [axis_datum_readability] good: J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible. | bad: Arm links are generic cylinders with no joint service evidence.
[cable_spine_serviceability] good: Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels. | bad: Cable routing is buried inside closed links.
[joint_cartridge_replaceability] good: Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links. | bad: Reducers/bearings are fused into structural links.

[bearing_shaft_mounting_sanity] good: Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races. | bad: Bearing dummies float in links with no seat, shoulder, retaining ring groove, or axial location scheme.

[moving_joint_harness_workmanship] good: Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range. | bad: Cables cross joints taut, pass through pinch zones, or have no relief at link entry/exit.

verify.value: all required fields present
encoder cover flange visible
cable exit hole present

generated_parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
generated_parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Driver board bay, power/signal separation along the arm

verify.value: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels.
verify.rule: [power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry. | bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.

verify.value: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme.
verify.rule: [return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals. | bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.

verify.value: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls.
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns. | bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value

risk.mit: Use threadlocker and torque to specification.
Implement thermal monitoring and shutdown.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Driver board bay, power/signal separation along the arm
physics_paths: Motor power lines run through Power Channel to Motor Junction Box
Sensor/encoder signals run through Signal Channel to Control Unit
Heat generated by drivers dissipates through Thermal Inspection Window and Chassis
integration_opportunities: Thermal management integration with Chassis cooling system

manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures required during LPBF for internal cavities
